title: Lasiocephalus otophorus
intro: Lasiocephalus otophorus, also known as 'Cordia', is a plant species belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae), native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to treat conditions such as fever, wounds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Lasiocephalus otophorus include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Lasiocephalus otophorus include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat various health conditions and to promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Lasiocephalus otophorus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Lasiocephalus, which is a group of plants that are commonly known as the "air plant" or "resurrection plant".
